data.title: American Axle & Manufacturing Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.06 beats by $0.10, revenue of $1.38B misses by $10M
data.description:
• American Axle & Manufacturing press release (NYSE:AXL): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.06 beats by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $1.38B (-5.5% Y/Y) misses by $10M.

  • AAM's full year 2025 financial targets are as follows:

    • AAM is targeting sales in the range of $5.8- $6.05 billion.
    • AAM is targeting Adjusted EBITDA in the range of $700 - $760 million.
    • AAM is targeting Adjusted free cash flow in the range of $200 - $230 million; this target assumes capital spending of approximately 5% of sales.

data.title: DaVita extends post-Q4 drop as Berkshire Hathaway cuts stake
data.description:
• DaVita (NYSE:DVA) continued to trade lower in the premarket on Friday as an insider sale from its major shareholder Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.A) (NYSE:BRK.B) worsened a selloff caused by the company’s 2025 outlook issued with its Q4 2024 results.
  • According to an SEC filing on Thursday, the Omaha, Nebraska-based conglomerate led by billionaire investor Warren Buffett has sold approximately 203.1K shares of dialysis services provider for $156.01 apiece.
  • With the Tuesday's transaction worth approximately $31.7M, Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.A), an owner with 10% or more holdings of DaVita (NYSE:DVA), has cut its stake in the company by ~1%.
  • Denver, Colorado-based DVA was a notable decliner in the postmarket trading on Thursday after the company set its 2025 adjusted earnings outlook at $10.20-$11.30 per share, below $11.44 per share in the consensus.

data.title: Owens Corning to sell glass reinforcements business to Praana Group for $755 million
data.description:
• Owens Corning (NYSE:OC) and Praana Group on Friday said they have signed a definitive agreement for the sale of Owens Corning’s glass reinforcements business to Praana Group at $755 million.
  • The transaction is expected to close in 2025.
  • Owens Corning’s glass reinforcements business generated revenue of about $1.1 billion in 2024 and has approximately 4,000 employees across a global footprint that includes 18 operations in 12 countries.
  • OC +0.53% premarket to $179.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Intel-Taiwan Semiconductor likely 'won't work,' Citi says
data.description:
Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) shares have gained nearly 25% over the past five trading sessions, in part due to speculation that the semiconductor giant could enter into some sort of tie up with Taiwan Semiconductor (NYSE:TSM) for its foundry operations.

And while such a deal may be batted around in Washington, D.C. because of geopolitical concerns, investment firm Citi believes it “won't work” for three key reasons.

“First, Intel employees would have to subjugate themselves to TSMC employees and directions,” Citi analyst Christopher Danely wrote in a note to clients. “Second, Intel and TSMC have completely different manufacturing process ecosystems and methodologies so Intel employees would have to learn completely different ways of manufacturing.”

“Lastly, Intel workers would likely have to adopt completely foreign working arrangements that exist in Taiwan such as living near the factory, being able to come to the factory at a moment’s notice in the middle of the night if needed, etc,” Danely added.

Danely kept his Neutral rating on Intel and reiterated his stance that the company should exit the foundry business and keep its focus on its “core microprocessor competency.”

The Santa Clara, Calif.-based chip giant has struggled over the years to turn itself around and compete with Taiwan Semiconductor and other global foundries, as it looks to cash in on the boom for making processors for other companies.

In its most recent quarter, Intel Foundry generated $4.5B in revenue, largely from Intel's own design arm. The company announced in September that it would manufacture some chips for Amazon (AMZN) Web Services.

The majority of AI chips are made by Taiwan Semiconductor and, to a lesser extent, Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF). Taiwan Semiconductor has a fab in Arizona and has spoken to Nvidia (NVDA) about making its Blackwell line of GPUs at the Arizona plant.

data.title: Fannie Mae posts Q4 earnings rise, revenue slips, delinquencies increase
data.description:
Fannie Mae (OTCQB:FNMA) stock slid 3.8% in Friday premarket trading after the government-sponsored enterprise posted a $321M provision for credit losses in Q4 as serious delinquency rates increased for both single-family and multifamily loans.

Q4 comprehensive income of $4.13B rose from $4.05B in Q3.

Q4 revenue of $7.30B declined from $7.34B in the prior quarter. Q4 net interest income of $7.18B fell 1% Q/Q.

Fanne Mae (OTCQB:FNMA) Q4 provision for credit losses of $321M vs. benefit of $27M in Q3.

Net investment losses of $10M reversed from net investment gain of $12M in the previous quarter.

Net worth climbed to $94.7B from $90.5B at Sept. 30, 2024.

"Our strong results were driven by guaranty fee income, consistent with the transformation of our business model that began well over a decade ago," said President and CEO Priscilla Almodovar. "For the year, we provided $381 billion in liquidity to the U.S. housing market, helping 1.4 million households buy, refinance, or rent a home."

Administrative expenses of $947M increased from $925M in the prior quarter.

During the year, Fannie Mae (OTCQB:FNMA) acquired ~778,000 single-family purchase loans, of which about half were first time homebuyers, and ~204,000 of single-family refinance loans.

In Q4, the U.S. weekly average 30-year fixed-rate mortgage rate rose to 6.85% at the end of 2024 from 6.08% in Q3.

The single-family serious delinquency rate increased to 0.56% from 0.52% in Q2 and 0.55% in Q4 2023. The multifamily serious delinquency rate of 0.57% ticked up from 0.56% in the prior quarter and 0.46% a year ago.

Conference call at 8:00 AM ET.

Earlier, Federal National Mortgage Association reports Q4 results

data.title: Matthews International to sell remaining businesses within SGK for $50M
data.description:
• Matthews International (NASDAQ:MATW) on Friday entered into a letter of intent (LOI) for the sale of the remaining operating businesses within the SGK Brand Solutions reporting segment, which primarily consists of its European roto-gravure packaging and surfaces businesses.
  • Under the terms of the LOI, Matthews will receive $50 million of total consideration, predominantly in cash which will be applied directly to the repayment of debt.
  • Earlier this month, Matthews entered into a definitive agreement under which it will receive $350 million of total upfront consideration in connection with the sale of its interest in SGK.
  • “The sale of the remaining operating businesses within SGK is another step forward toward a more streamlined business structure and further evidence of our commitment to unlocking the value of our businesses,” said Joseph Bartolacci, Chief Executive Officer of Matthews. “This transaction provides us with additional cash to continue paying down debt. When combined with the sale of these remaining European operating businesses within the SGK reporting segment, the total upfront consideration for the sale of SGK will be $400 million, implying a total current value of the segment in excess of $600 million. Once the new entity of our previously announced transaction is fully synergized and we dispose of our investment in our operating software, we expect the total realized value of the divestiture of this segment to exceed $700 million.”
  • The transaction is expected to be completed in the third quarter, the company said.
  • MATW +4.08% premarket to $25.99.
  • Source: Press Release
